title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: fit.surr() expects data long format one row per subject-time measurement. Key requirements enforced code: id identifies subjects; must one observation per subject-time combination. treat indicates treatment assignment; coerced factor intended represent two treatment levels. time must numeric equally spaced across observed time points. time omitted, function creates within-subject index Time assuming data already ordered equally spaced. surrogate design must make treatment linear combination surrogate terms; otherwise conditional model identifiable.

what: Package functions used in this vignette
title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: marginal model producing total treatment effects Δ(t)\Delta(t) conditional model (given surrogate) producing residual treatment effects ΔR(t)\Delta_R(t) stores bootstrap draws corresponding fixed-effect parameters. Local PTE: LPTE(t)=1−ΔR(t)/Δ(t)\text{LPTE}(t) = 1 - \Delta_R(t)/\Delta(t) Cumulative PTE: CPTE(t)=1−∑h≤tΔR(h)/∑h≤tΔ(h)\text{CPTE}(t) = 1 - \sum_{h\le t} \Delta_R(h) / \sum_{h\le t} \Delta(h) Treatment effects Δ(t)\Delta(t) ΔR(t)\Delta_R(t) time_homo_test() tests hypothesis PTE constant time (implemented via max-type statistic Monte Carlo approximation null).

previous_headings: Package functions used in this vignette
title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: fit.surr() requires: formula: outcome mean model. function internally add treatment--time fixed effects. id: subject identifier (unquoted). treat: treatment variable (unquoted). surrogate: surrogate structure (formula string). time: numeric time variable (unquoted). formulas fixed effects surrogate structures accept temporal structure available kDGLM package (see vignette details). Functions transform data also supported. particular, provide lagged function, computes lagged values arguments can included model formula account delayed lingering effects predictor time. also provide s function, generates spline basis numeric variable can used model smooth, potentially non-linear effects without specify basis expansion manually.
code: library(OnlineSurr) head(sim_onlinesurr) #>   id trt time          s         y #> 1  1   0    1 0.02116371 0.3072608 #> 2  1   0    2 0.37008517 0.7649493 #> 3  1   0    3 0.63126012 1.0500510 #> 4  1   0    4 0.79780239 1.0951569 #> 5  1   0    5 1.06470750 1.5650095 #> 6  1   0    6 1.24374405 1.4326703  fit <- fit.surr(   formula   = y ~ 1, # baseline fixed effects; trt*time terms added internally   id        = id,   surrogate = ~s, # surrogate structure   treat     = trt,   data      = sim_onlinesurr,   time      = time,   N.boots   = 2000, # bootstrap draws stored in the fitted object   verbose   = 0 # hide progress ) library(OnlineSurr)  fit <- fit.surr(   formula   = y ~ 1, # baseline fixed effects; trt*time terms added internally   id        = id,   surrogate = ~ s(s) + s(lagged(s, 1)) + s(lagged(s, 2)), # surrogate structure   treat     = trt,   data      = sim_onlinesurr,   time      = time,   verbose   = 0 # hide progress )

previous_headings: Package functions used in this vignette > Fitting the models with fit.surr()
title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: returned object class fitted_onlinesurr list (least): fit$T: number time points fit$N: number subjects fit$n.fixed: number fixed-effect coefficients per subject design (reference size) fit$Marginal$point: point estimates (vector) marginal model fit$Marginal$smp: bootstrap draws (matrix) marginal model fit$Conditional$point: point estimates (vector) conditional model fit$Conditional$smp: bootstrap draws (matrix) conditional model first T (practice, first n.fixed) elements used plotting/testing methods correspond time-indexed treatment-effect parameters.

title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: package provides S3 summary method summary.fitted_onlinesurr(). t selects time index. cumulative=TRUE reports cumulative effects time t (implemented method). cumulative=FALSE reports time-specific quantities time t .
code: summary(fit, t = 6, cumulative = TRUE) #> Fitted Online Surrogate #>  #> Cummulated effects at time 6: #>         Estimate Std. Error t value   Pr(>|t|)    #> Delta    8.99606  0.05060   177.77382 0.0000e+00 *** #> Delta.R  2.99490  0.48610     6.16108 7.2251e-10 *** #> CPTE     0.66709  0.05387       -         -        #>  #> Time homogeneity test:  #>  #> Test stat.   Crit. value   p-value      #>    1.05798       2.44403    0.65432     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

what: Plotting LPTE, CPTE, and treatment effects
title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: plot() dispatches plot.fitted_onlinesurr().    Interpretation notes: LPTE measures, time, proportion total treatment effect explained surrogate, using ratio 1−ΔR(t)/Δ(t)1 - \Delta_R(t)/\Delta(t). CPTE aggregates effects time tt, using cumulative sums.
code: plot(fit, type = "LPTE") # Local PTE over time plot(fit, type = "CPTE") # Cumulative PTE over time plot(fit, type = "Delta") # Delta and Delta_R over time

title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: time_homo_test() provides max-type test, using Monte Carlo approximation null distribution. Returned components: T: observed test statistic T.crit: critical value requested significance level p.value: Monte Carlo p-value
code: test <- time_homo_test(fit, signif.level = 0.05, N.boots = 50000) test #> $T #> [1] 1.05798 #>  #> $T.crit #>      95%  #> 2.446708  #>  #> $p.value #> [1] 0.65408

title: OnlineSurr: Fitting marginal/conditional models and computing LPTE/CPTE
text: Time index must numeric equally spaced. missing measurements, include missing time points NA outcomes rather dropping times, spacing remains consistent. One row per subject-time. duplicates, aggregate first (e.g., average within time window) decide measurement keep. Bootstrap size tradeoff.fit.surr(N.boots=...) controls stored bootstrap draws used confidence intervals associated treatment effect, LPTE CPTE; time_homo_test(N.boots=...) controls Monte Carlo draws null distribution time homogeneity test. See Santos Jr. Parast (2026) details theoretical aspects package.

what: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
title: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
text: OnlineSurr R package surrogate evaluation primary outcome surrogate marker measured longitudinally. package implements state-space approach based methodology developed Causal Framework Evaluating Jointly Longitudinal Outcomes Surrogate Markers: State-Space Approach. current implementation fits two Gaussian state-space models: marginal model longitudinal outcome function treatment time; conditional model additionally adjusts user-specified surrogate structure. two fitted models, package produces time-specific treatment-effect estimates, bootstrap-based uncertainty summaries, estimates local cumulative proportion treatment effect explained (LPTE CPTE), test temporal homogeneity PTE.

title: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
text: package yet CRAN. Install development version GitHub : Depending local setup, may also need install package dependencies first.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("silvaneojunior/OnlineSurr")

title: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
text: current source imports functionality : kDGLM dplyr tidyr rlang ggplot2 Rfast latex2exp

title: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
text: Go view tutorial package: OnlineSurr vignette See Santos Jr. Parast (2026) details theoretical aspects package.
code: library(OnlineSurr)  fit <- fit.surr(   formula   = y ~ 1,   id        = id,   surrogate = ~ s1 + s2,   treat     = trt,   data      = dat,   time      = time,   N.boots   = 500 )  summary(fit, t = fit$T, cumulative = TRUE) plot(fit, type = "LPTE") plot(fit, type = "CPTE") plot(fit, type = "Delta") time_homo_test(fit)

title: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
text: current stage, package focused core methodology assumes user supplies appropriate surrogate specification. practice, users pay careful attention : choice surrogate history lag structure; whether treatment effects well defined time point; study design assumptions required causal interpretation. current implementation best viewed research package accompanying methodological paper.

title: Surrogate Evaluation for Jointly Longitudinal Outcome and Surrogate
text: use repository, please cite associated paper:
code: Santos Jr., S. V. dos, and Parast, L. (2026). A causal framework for evaluating jointly longitudinal outcomes and surrogate markers: A state-space approach.

what: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
text: Fits two Gaussian state-space models (Dynamic Linear Models) jointly longitudinal outcome data: () marginal model outcome trajectory given treatment time, (ii) conditional model additionally adjusts user-specified surrogate structure. function returns per-time treatment-effect estimates models subject-level bootstrap draws obtained via subject-level resampling.

title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
code: fit.surr(   formula,   id,   surrogate,   treat,   data = NULL,   time = NULL,   N.boots = 2000,   verbose = 1,   D.local = 0.8 )

title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
text: formula object class formula describing fixed-effects mean structure primary outcome. left-hand side must outcome variable. Internally, right-hand side augmented include treatment--time fixed effects. id variable (unquoted) identifying subjects. subject must one measurement per time value. surrogate formula describing surrogate structure included conditional model. May provided either formula (e.g., ~ s1 + s2) string can coerced formula. treat variable (unquoted) indicating treatment assignment. Must encode exactly two treatment levels coercion factor. data data.frame containing variables referenced formula, id, treat, surrogate, (optionally) time. time Optional variable (unquoted) giving measurement time index. Must numeric equally spaced across observed time points. NULL, equally spaced within-subject index created current row order (warning). N.boots Integer number subject-level bootstrap replicates. replicate resamples subjects replacement recombines subject-specific sufficient quantities form bootstrap draws fixed effects. verbose Logical scalar indicating whether print progress information model fitting. TRUE, progress updates shown; FALSE, progress output produced. D.local Numeric, number 0 1 indicating discount factor used random effect block. factor controls smooth random effect evolve time. discount factor 1 means random effects change time, individual local level, level times. discount factor 0 acceptable (kDGLM package replace 1), values closer 0 imply flexible dynamic. See West Harrison (1997)  appendix dos Santos Jr. Parast (2026)  instructions specify discount factor.

title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
text: object class "fitted_onlinesurr": named list elements $Marginal $Conditional. contains: point: point estimate vector treatment effect time point. smp: matrix bootstrap draws treatment effect time point, one column per bootstrap replicate. draws generated joint distribution full vector, thereby accounting dependence among different time points. samples marginal (total effect) conditional (residual effect) models paired, -th samples models drawn jointly distribution estimators. object also includes: T: number unique time points. N: number subjects. n.fixed: number fixed-effect coefficients implied formula single subject prior stacking across subjects.

title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
text: implementation follows two-model decomposition used estimating longitudinal treatment effects surrogate-adjusted (residual) treatment effects state-space framework. See dos Santos Jr. Parast (2026)  details methodology. See West Harrison (1997)  best practices model specification state-space model setting. Data requirements. data must one row per subject-time pair; time must numeric equally spaced (omitted, case index created). Treatment subject identifiers coerced factors sorted levels. Model structure. marginal model includes treatment--time fixed effects subject-specific random-walk component capture within-subject correlation. conditional model adds user-specified surrogate structure design, checks treatment linear combination surrogate design (rank check). Bootstrap. Subjects resampled replacement. Subject-specific filtered quantities computed recombined bootstrap iteration reduce computational cost, consistent subject-level nonparametric bootstrap strategy replicated time series.

title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
text: Silvaneo V. dos Santos Jr., Layla Parast (2026). “Causal Framework Evaluating Jointly Longitudinal Outcomes Surrogate Markers: State-Space Approach.” 2604.12882, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12882. Mike West, Jeff Harrison (1997). Bayesian Forecasting Dynamic Models (Springer Series Statistics). Springer-Verlag. ISBN 0387947256.

title: Fit marginal and conditional state-space models for longitudinal surrogate evaluation — fit.surr
code: fit <- fit.surr(y ~ 1,   id = id,   surrogate = ~s,   treat = trt,   data = sim_onlinesurr, # This dataset is included in the OnlineSurr package   time = time,   verbose = 0,   N.boots = 500 # Generally, this value would be too small.   # Remember to increase it for your dataset. ) summary(fit) #> Fitted Online Surrogate #>  #> Cummulated effects at time 6: #>         Estimate Std. Error t value   Pr(>|t|)    #> Delta    8.99606  0.04861   185.06592 0.0000e+00 *** #> Delta.R  2.99490  0.47800     6.26547 3.7169e-10 *** #> CPTE     0.66709  0.05306       -         -        #>  #> Time homogeneity test:  #>  #> Test stat.   Crit. value   p-value      #>    1.11091       2.43248    0.62236     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>

title: Compute a spline configuration — get.config.s
text: Derive configuration needed evaluate s() given input vector. helper resolves boundary limits, chooses validates knot locations, augments knot vector repeated boundary knots, returns arguments form can used rebuild update spline calls.

title: Compute a spline configuration — get.config.s
code: get.config.s(   x,   P = 3,   K = min(7, max(3, floor(log2(length(unique(x)))))),   limits = c(NA, NA),   knots = "eq" )

title: Compute a spline configuration — get.config.s
text: x numeric vector predictor values. P non-negative integer giving spline degree. P = 3 corresponds cubic B-spline basis. K integer giving number basis functions implied spline specification. limits numeric vector length 2 giving lower upper boundary limits spline basis. Missing values replaced min(x) max(x). knots Either numeric vector knot locations, one "eq" "quantile". "eq", knots placed uniformly limits[1] limits[2]. "quantile", knots placed equally spaced empirical quantiles x.

title: Compute a spline configuration — get.config.s
text: named list containing: x unevaluated expression supplied x, returned via substitute(x). knots full augmented knot vector, including repeated boundary knots. limits resolved lower upper boundary limits. P spline degree. K number basis functions.

title: Compute a spline configuration — get.config.s
text: function intended programmatic use, example rewriting model formulas contain calls s(...).

what: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
title: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
text: Produces ggplot2 figure showing, time, either Local PTE (LPTE), Cumulative PTE (CPTE), marginal residual treatment effects \(\Delta(t)\) \(\Delta_R(t)\) (labeled \(\Delta\) \(\Delta_R\) plot). Point estimates taken object$Marginal$point object$Conditional$point, uncertainty bands computed stored bootstrap draws.

title: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
code: # S3 method for class 'fitted_onlinesurr' plot(x, type = "LPTE", conf.level = 0.95, one.sided = TRUE, ...)

title: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
text: x "fitted_onlinesurr" object, typically returned fit.surr. must contain $T, $n.fixed, components $Marginal $Conditional, point smp. type Character string specifying plot. One "LPTE", "CPTE", "Delta" (case-insensitive). "Delta" plots \(\Delta(t)\) \(\Delta_R(t)\) separate colors. conf.level Numeric \((0,1)\) giving confidence level plotted intervals. Default 0.95. one.sided Logical; TRUE (default), uses signif.level = (1-conf.level)/2 taking quantiles, tail excludes 1-conf.level (.e., wider interval usual two-sided conf.level interval). convenient visually assessing one-sided surrogate validation criteria. FALSE, uses standard two-sided construction signif.level = 1-conf.level. ... Additional arguments (currently unused) included S3 method compatibility.

title: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
text: function extracts time-indexed treatment-effect estimates \(\Delta(t)\) (marginal) \(\Delta_R(t)\) (residual/conditional) fitted object, along bootstrap draws . constructs: LPTE: \(\mathrm{LPTE}(t) = 1 - \Delta_R(t)/\Delta(t)\). CPTE: \(\mathrm{CPTE}(t) = 1 - \sum_{u\le t}\Delta_R(u)/\sum_{u\le t}\Delta(u)\). Delta: plots \(\Delta(t)\) \(\Delta_R(t)\) directly. Point estimates plotted points; intervals empirical quantile intervals computed bootstrap sample matrices stored object.

title: Plot time-varying PTE measures and treatment effects from a
code: fit <- fit.surr(y ~ 1,   id = id,   surrogate = ~s,   treat = trt,   data = sim_onlinesurr, # This dataset is included in the OnlineSurr package   time = time,   verbose = 0,   N.boots = 500 # Generally, this value would be too small.   # Remember to increase it for your dataset. )  plot(fit, type = "LPTE")  plot(fit, type = "CPTE", conf.level = 0.90, one.sided = FALSE)  plot(fit, type = "Delta")

what: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
title: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
text: Build B-spline basis numeric vector using Cox-de Boor style recursion. default, function constructs cubic spline basis (P = 3) chooses number basis functions number unique values x.

title: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
code: s(   x,   P = 3,   K = min(7, max(3, floor(log2(length(unique(x)))))),   limits = c(NA, NA),   knots = "eq" )

title: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
text: x numeric vector predictor values. P non-negative integer giving spline degree. P = 3 corresponds cubic B-spline basis. K integer giving number basis functions return. default increases slowly number unique values x. limits numeric vector length 2 giving lower upper boundary limits spline basis. Missing values replaced min(x) max(x). knots Either numeric vector knot locations, one "eq" "quantile". "eq", knots placed uniformly limits[1] limits[2]. "quantile", knots placed equally spaced empirical quantiles x.

title: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
text: numeric matrix one row per element x one column per spline basis function.

title: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
text: Boundary limits taken x unless supplied explicitly. Knot locations may given directly numeric vector, generated either equally spaced locations ("eq") empirical quantiles ("quantile"). returned basis length(x) rows k columns. knots generated internally, function first creates K - P + 1 knot locations augments repeated boundary knots recursion can evaluated.

title: Construct a B-spline basis matrix — s
code: x <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 10)  # Default cubic basis B <- s(x) dim(B) #> [1] 10  3  # Equally spaced knots with custom basis size B2 <- s(x, K = 5, knots = "eq")  # Quantile-based knots B3 <- s(x, knots = "quantile")

title: Simulated longitudinal surrogate dataset for `OnlineSurr` — sim_onlinesurr
text: data frame 600 rows 5 variables: id Integer subject identifier. trt Binary treatment indicator: `0` control `1` treated. time Numeric measurement time index taking values `1` `6`. s Continuous longitudinal surrogate marker. y Continuous longitudinal primary outcome.

title: Simulated longitudinal surrogate dataset for `OnlineSurr` — sim_onlinesurr
text: Simulated data generated within package; based external study.

title: Simulated longitudinal surrogate dataset for `OnlineSurr` — sim_onlinesurr
text: dataset contains 100 subjects observed 6 equally spaced time points. Treatment assignment binary constant within subject. surrogate marker `s` varies time affected treatment. primary outcome `y` depends treatment, time, surrogate marker. Rows ordered subject identifier time. dataset generated package examples testing. represents balanced longitudinal design one observation per subject-time pair. Measurement times equally spaced, requirement use [fit.surr()]. data-generating mechanism, surrogate marker affected time treatment, outcome depends time, treatment, surrogate.

title: Summarize a
text: Prints human-readable report object class "fitted_onlinesurr" returned fit.surr. report includes marginal conditional treatment-effect estimates selected time point (cumulatively time), estimate LPTE/CPTE, time-homogeneity test LPTE.

title: Summarize a
code: # S3 method for class 'fitted_onlinesurr' summary(object, t = object$T, cumulative = TRUE, signif.level = 0.05, ...)

title: Summarize a
text: object "fitted_onlinesurr" object. t Integer time index evaluate treatment effects PTE. cumulative = TRUE, effects aggregated times 1:t. cumulative = FALSE, effects evaluated time t . cumulative Logical; TRUE (default), report uses cumulative (time t) marginal conditional treatment effects. FALSE, report uses effects time t . signif.level Numeric \((0,1)\) giving significance level time-homogeneity test reported (e.g., via time_homo_test). ... Additional arguments passed downstream summary/print utilities ().

title: Summarize a
text: return value. Called side effect printing summary report.

title: Summarize a
text: "fitted_onlinesurr" object stores point estimates bootstrap samples marginal surrogate-adjusted (conditional) models object$Marginal object$Conditional.

title: Summarize a
code: fit <- fit.surr(y ~ 1,   id = id,   surrogate = ~s,   treat = trt,   data = sim_onlinesurr, # This dataset is included in the OnlineSurr package   time = time,   verbose = 0,   N.boots = 500 # Generally, this value would be too small.   # Remember to increase it for your dataset. )  # Cumulative up to time 5 summary(fit, t = 5, cumulative = TRUE, signif.level = 0.05) #> Fitted Online Surrogate #>  #> Cummulated effects at time 5: #>         Estimate Std. Error t value   Pr(>|t|)    #> Delta    6.65540  0.04904   135.72003 0.0000e+00 *** #> Delta.R  2.27557  0.35247     6.45608 1.0745e-10 *** #> CPTE     0.65809  0.05284       -         -        #>  #> Time homogeneity test:  #>  #> Test stat.   Crit. value   p-value      #>    1.11084       2.43794    0.61612     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>   # Time-specific at time 5 summary(fit, t = 5, cumulative = FALSE) #> Fitted Online Surrogate #>  #> Local effects at time 5: #>         Estimate Std. Error t value  Pr(>|t|)    #> Delta    2.01395  0.02172   92.71419 0.0000e+00 *** #> Delta.R  0.62847  0.11065    5.67994 1.3474e-08 *** #> LPTE     0.68794  0.05494      -         -        #>  #> Time homogeneity test:  #>  #> Test stat.   Crit. value   p-value      #>    1.11084       2.43875    0.61710     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>

what: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
text: Tests null hypothesis LPTE constant time. test based difference conditional marginal treatment-effect trajectories implied fitted "fitted_onlinesurr" object, standardized estimated covariance, uses max-type statistic control family wise error across time points.

title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
code: time_homo_test(model, signif.level = 0.05, N.boots = 50000)

title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
text: model fitted object class "fitted_onlinesurr", typically returned fit.surr. Must contain $T, $n.fixed, elements $Marginal $Conditional point smp components. signif.level Numeric (0,1) giving test significance level used form critical value bootstrap distribution. Default 0.05. N.boots Integer number Monte Carlo draws used approximate null distribution max standardized deviation statistic compute p-value. Default 50000.

title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
text: named list : T: observed test statistic (maximum absolute standardized deviation). T.crit: 1-signif.level critical value. p.value: Monte Carlo p-value mean(T_null > T_obs).

title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
text: See dos Santos Jr. Parast (2026)  theoretical details test. Notes: function assumes first T time-specific treatment-effect parameters stored contiguously beginning model$Marginal$point model$Conditional$point (similarly smp). uses index 1:(n.fixed) implemented code: 1:(T + n.fixed - T). N.boots Monte Carlo size null simulation (distinct bootstrap size used fitting model).

title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
text: Silvaneo V. dos Santos Jr., Layla Parast (2026). “Causal Framework Evaluating Jointly Longitudinal Outcomes Surrogate Markers: State-Space Approach.” 2604.12882, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12882.

title: Test time-homogeneity of the PTE — time_homo_test
code: fit <- fit.surr(y ~ 1,   id = id,   surrogate = ~s,   treat = trt,   data = sim_onlinesurr, # This dataset is included in the OnlineSurr package   time = time,   verbose = 0,   N.boots = 500 # Generally, this value would be too small.   # Remember to increase it for your dataset. )  time_homo_test(fit, signif.level = 0.05, N.boots = 500) #> $T #> [1] 1.058295 #>  #> $T.crit #>      95%  #> 2.341364  #>  #> $p.value #> [1] 0.654 #>
